summary: A trailer-style promo framed like a movie: it opens on a cinematic aerial drone shot tracking the border wall, splices in a repurposed CNN interview of a masked 'cartel leader' warning 'once you get in, you can't get out' and news B-roll of migrants crossing a river, then resolves to a film-style title card reading 'CBP PRESENTS ... THE MOST SECURE BORDER IN HISTORY.' The 'CBP presents' framing, dramatic drone cinematography, and repurposed cartel/news clips package an enforcement message as a cinematic trailer aimed at spectacle, fitting produced content, though metadata flags no audio track.

script.scene: Cinematic aerial drone shot tracking the steel border wall stretching toward mountains, with a Mexican border town on one side and a road/rail line on the U.S. side; sweeping establishing tone.
Repurposed CNN clip interior of an SUV: a masked man in sunglasses and tactical vest (a 'cartel leader') sits for an interview; LIVE bug visible.
CNN two-shot, reporter facing the masked cartel figure; chyron 'CARTEL LEADER WARNS: ONCE YOU GET IN, YOU CAN'T GET OUT.'
News B-roll of a group of migrants wading across a river between brushy banks with scattered debris on the shore.
Final film-style title card over a bright drone shot of the border wall and open road: 'CBP PRESENTS / In collaboration with USBP and partners / THE MOST SECURE BORDER IN HISTORY.'

notable_text: CBP PRESENTS; In collaboration with USBP and partners; THE MOST SECURE BORDER IN HISTORY; CNN chyron 'CARTEL LEADER WARNS: ONCE YOU GET IN, YOU CAN'T GET OUT'; tweet: 'Making life harder for cartel members, it's what we do.'
